description: Test structural graph patterns: negative-cost cycles, piecewise-linear parallel arcs, sparse multi-hop, single-unit, max-flow, large uint256 capacity.
steps: `TestStructural_NegativeCostCycleInGraph`: 4-node graph with arcs forming a negative-cost cycle (0->1 cost -5; 1->2 cost -5; 2->0 cost -5; plus src/sink arcs). Assert finite optimum matching hand-computed value; checkSolution.

`TestStructural_PiecewiseLinearParallelArcs`: 3 parallel arcs u->v costs [1,3,7] equal capacities; push flow requiring all three; assert saturation in cost-ascending order by inspecting Flow on each arc post-solve.
`TestStructural_SparseMultiHop`: 10-node chain 0->1->...->9 with cap 1 cost 1 per arc; src=0 sink=9 send 1 unit. Assert TotalCost == 9, every arc Flow == 1; checkSolution.
`TestStructural_SingleUnitFlow`: graph where optimum is 1 unit through a single path. Assert TotalFlow equals uint256.NewInt(1).
`TestStructural_MaxFlow`: graph where total supply matches min-cut capacity; assert every arc on a min-cut has Flow == Capacity.

`TestStructural_LargeUint256Capacity`: 3-node graph with capacities `new(uint256.Int).Lsh(uint256.NewInt(1), 200)`; assert Result.TotalFlow.Cmp(new(uint256.Int).SetUint64(math.MaxUint64)) > 0 (uint256 path exercised, not truncated).
acceptance_criteria: structural_test.go defines the six tests listed.
TestStructural_NegativeCostCycleInGraph produces a finite optimum without special handling.
TestStructural_LargeUint256Capacity asserts TotalFlow > MaxUint64 via uint256.Cmp.
